jokes.text: Don't eat pork, Not even with a fork, Can't touch this
jokes.setup: Springfield's answer to the Benedictine monks: The rapping rabbis
jokes.punchline: Combining religious dietary laws with MC Hammer lyrics
jokes.explanation: Clever mashup of religious observance with 90s hip-hop, economical and memorable

jokes.text: Marge, are we Jewish? No, Homer.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about his own religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his obliviousness to basic facts about his own life

jokes.text: But first, we all stink. We all... Hey!
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's reading something insulting about himself
jokes.explanation: Classic teleprompter gag with good timing and character reaction

jokes.text: I'm telling you people, the Earth revolves around the sun.
jokes.setup: Springfield ranked dead last in science
jokes.punchline: Basic astronomical fact treated as controversial
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of anti-intellectualism, timeless and well-crafted

jokes.text: Oh, that's right. I'm crippled.
jokes.setup: FDR actor proposing to do something about depression
jokes.punchline: Suddenly remembering his physical limitation
jokes.explanation: Dark but well-crafted historical joke with perfect timing and character awareness

jokes.text: Maps to movie stars' homes!
jokes.punchline: Moe's pathetic attempt at tourist attraction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's delusional schemes

jokes.text: Excuse me, are you Drew Barrymore? What? Get out of here. I'm hung-over. Sorry, Miss Barrymore. What?
jokes.setup: Tourist asking if someone is Drew Barrymore
jokes.punchline: It actually IS Drew Barrymore, hungover and hostile
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection gag with good celebrity satire

jokes.text: So we propose changing our name from Springfield to Seinfeld.
jokes.setup: Easiest way to be popular is to leech off popularity of others
jokes.punchline: Literal application to popular TV show
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about TV shows and popularity, very quotable and self-aware

jokes.text: Young man, that appears to be a picture of your rear end.
jokes.setup: Bart showing slides of 'Springfield from space'
jokes.punchline: Slide is actually Bart's butt
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart prank with good visual comedy setup

jokes.text: So long, suckers. This is the last time I use an escape plan devised by Milhouse. Sorry, Bart.
jokes.punchline: Blaming Milhouse for the failed escape plan
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics and blame-shifting humor

jokes.text: The metric system is the tool of the devil!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting past idea about metric system
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's extreme anti-metric reaction
jokes.explanation: Iconic Grandpa line, highly quotable and perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: My car gets 40 rods to the hogshead, and that's the way I likes it!
jokes.punchline: Using completely made-up old-timey measurements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist measurement that sounds authentically old-timey while being completely nonsensical

jokes.text: The old person's remarks will be stricken. Who said that?
jokes.punchline: Official dismissal followed by Grandpa's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the Grandpa joke effectively

jokes.text: Don't push your luck! Don't push your luck!
jokes.setup: Marge suggests she has several other ideas
jokes.punchline: Crowd's immediate rejection of more Marge ideas
jokes.explanation: Good crowd reaction that shows Marge's history with unpopular suggestions

jokes.text: Homer in the Shower and Homer on the Toilet
jokes.punchline: Previous films are just Homer in bathroom situations
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart humor with exploitation of Homer's privacy

jokes.text: Relaxed fit, my Aunt Fanny. Stupid Dockers.
jokes.punchline: Cursing the clothing brand's sizing claims
jokes.explanation: Relatable Homer frustration with good brand-specific humor

jokes.text: Lights, camera...act-diddily-dotilly-doodilly action Jackson.
jokes.punchline: Flanders-speak version of 'lights, camera, action'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders speech pattern applied to film direction

jokes.text: Flanders to God, Flanders to God, Get off your cloud and save my Todd
jokes.setup: Todd is drowning in Flanders' biblical movie
jokes.punchline: Prayer styled like radio communication
jokes.explanation: Clever mixing of divine communication with radio protocol

jokes.text: Thanks, God. Okely-dokely.
jokes.punchline: God responds in Flanders-speak
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept that God would speak in Flanders' dialect, economical and memorable

jokes.text: Did you know there are over 600 critics on TV and Leonard Maltin is the best-looking of them all?
jokes.setup: Marge looking for a film critic
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Maltin's looks among critics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about the generally unphotogenic nature of film critics

jokes.text: I wish I was dead.
jokes.punchline: Critic's existential response to bad movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that captures critic's despair at bad sequels

jokes.text: Did you ever notice how men always leave the toilet seat up? That's the joke.
jokes.punchline: Weak stand-up premise presented as if it's brilliant
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about hacky comedy with McBain's action-hero delivery of observational humor

jokes.text: You suck, McBain!
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile audience reaction
jokes.explanation: Good crowd reaction that shows how bad McBain's comedy is

jokes.text: Now my Woody Allen impression. I'm a neurotic nerd who likes to sleep with little girls.
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark and inappropriate Woody Allen reference
jokes.explanation: Edgy celebrity joke that's more shock value than clever wit

jokes.text: Hey, that really sucked!
jokes.punchline: Even more hostile audience reaction
jokes.explanation: Predictable reaction beat that escalates the crowd hostility

jokes.text: The film is just me in front of a brick wall for an hour and a half. It cost $80 million.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously expensive minimalist film concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of pretentious indie films vs. Hollywood excess

jokes.text: How do you sleep at night? On top of a pile of money with many beautiful ladies.
jokes.setup: Moral question about making terrible expensive movies
jokes.punchline: Completely literal, materialistic answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with highly quotable, character-perfect response

jokes.text: No, my ears are really burning. I wanted to see inside, so I lit a Q-Tip.
jokes.setup: My ears are burning (usually means people talking about you)
jokes.punchline: Homer literally set fire to a Q-tip in his ear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - taking idiom literally with dangerous stupidity

jokes.text: Look what you made me write.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about pimple/boil while Marge is writing
jokes.punchline: Letter is presumably ruined by Homer's interruption
jokes.explanation: Good domestic comedy showing Homer's poor timing

jokes.text: Sherman, I just realized you insulted me. Now you will die.
jokes.setup: Jay's negative review of McBain's movie
jokes.punchline: McBain's delayed, violent reaction
jokes.explanation: Good McBain character moment with action-movie threat escalation

jokes.text: Hey, nudnik, your shoe's untied. From here, they appear to be tied. But I will go in for a closer look.
jokes.punchline: McBain's literal, careful examination of the situation
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing McBain's methodical, literal thinking

jokes.text: On closer inspection, these are Loafers.
jokes.punchline: Serious delivery of obvious shoe identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of unnecessary observation, highly quotable

jokes.text: I saw your hair from the plane.
jokes.setup: Meeting Marge for the first time
jokes.punchline: Her hair is visible from aircraft altitude
jokes.explanation: Great Marge hair joke with good visual comedy

jokes.text: It was a gummy bear.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that resolves earlier setup with typical Homer logic

jokes.text: I smell another cheap cartoon crossover.
jokes.punchline: Direct commentary on lazy animation industry practices
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on animation industry from The Critic character

jokes.text: I suddenly feel so dirty.
jokes.setup: Bart saying kids should watch Jay's show
jokes.punchline: Critic's horror at having child viewers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jay's self-awareness about his adult content

jokes.text: I ought to club them and eat their bones.
jokes.setup: Advisor saying people see Burns as an ogre
jokes.punchline: Burns' immediate violent reaction proves the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment that immediately confirms the image problem

jokes.text: A slick Hollywood picture to gloss over my evil rise to power like Bugsy or Working Girl.
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to criminals and including Working Girl
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire about Hollywood biopics whitewashing villains

jokes.text: Get his nonunion Mexican equivalent.
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual exploitation of workers
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns exploitation mentality with economic humor

jokes.text: Listen, Señor Spielbergo...
jokes.punchline: Fake Hispanic version of Spielberg's name
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to previous joke with ethnic stereotype humor

jokes.text: Schindler and I are like peas in a pod. We're both factory owners. We both made shells for the Nazis. But mine worked, damn it.
jokes.punchline: Revealing he actually helped Nazis effectively
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that perfectly captures Burns' evil with historical reference

jokes.text: Well, it's not like I carry them around with me. Oh, look, here's my People's Choice Award. Five Golden Globes... Where's my Emmy?
jokes.setup: Jay claiming he doesn't carry awards around
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts listing all his awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contradiction showing Jay's ego despite his protests

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I won the belching contest at work.
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic counterpoint achievement
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his inability to compete intellectually

jokes.text: Wow, how many Pulitzer Prize winners can do that? Just me and Eudora Welty.
jokes.punchline: Only other accomplished belcher is elderly female author
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected pairing of literary excellence with crude bodily function

jokes.text: So then I said to Woody Allen, 'Well, Camus can do, but Sartre is smartre.'
jokes.punchline: Philosophical pun delivered to famous director
jokes.explanation: Clever philosophical wordplay that shows Jay's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: Yeah, well, Scooby-Doo can doo-doo, but Jimmy Carter is smarter.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to match Jay's intellectual wordplay
jokes.punchline: Crude cartoon pun with random presidential reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer attempt at intellectualism that completely misses the mark but is highly quotable

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know. Harvey Fierstein.
jokes.setup: Homer asking who's gay in Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Obviously gay actor as safe, boring answer
jokes.explanation: Safe, predictable celebrity reference without much wit

jokes.text: MacGyver's gay.
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong assertion about action hero
jokes.explanation: Setup for MacGyver retaliation gag, shows Homer's random assertions

jokes.text: You badmouthed MacGyver, didn't you?
jokes.punchline: Twins' immediate violent defense of their crush
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the twins' MacGyver obsession and Homer's consequences

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Why did it take you so long to say yes?
jokes.setup: Marge saying she respects Homer's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Homer analyzing the timing of her response
jokes.explanation: Good escalating insecurity gag showing Homer's awareness of others' hesitation

jokes.text: Are you humoring me? / Yes. / Okay. Wait a minute. That's bad!
jokes.setup: Homer's continued questioning about Marge's responses
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes being humored is negative
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Homer's insecurity spiral with delayed realization

jokes.text: Oh, I wish I were an Oscar Mayer wiener, That is what I'd truly like to be, 'Cause if I were an Oscar Mayer wiener, Everyone would be in love with me
jokes.setup: Homer proving he knows the hot dog song
jokes.punchline: Singing the entire jingle perfectly
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer showing his mastery of trivial commercial knowledge

jokes.text: That's it, Marge. He knows the whole hot-dog song. Go ahead, sleep with him.
jokes.setup: Homer impressed by Jay's knowledge of Oscar Mayer jingle
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers wife to superior jingle-knower
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic escalation - commercial knowledge equals sexual worthiness

jokes.text: It's just you and me now, lock of hair. You don't have to do this.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically taking lock of Marge's hair
jokes.punchline: Talking to the hair, Jay trying to counsel him
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of Homer's jealousy with Jay as voice of reason

jokes.text: Oh, it's hopeless. I'll have to play myself.
jokes.punchline: Decides only he can play himself after rejecting exact copies
jokes.explanation: Good ironic conclusion to audition sequence

jokes.text: Help! Help! Police! Hey, I got problems of my own right now. Oh, boy. This is gonna get worse before it gets better.
jokes.punchline: Police chief too busy to help, pessimistic about outcome
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment showing police incompetence

jokes.text: Money gets you one more round / Drink it down, you stupid clown / Money gets you one more round / You're out on your ass
jokes.punchline: Harsh but rhythmic song about drinking economics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character voice - crude but practical bar philosophy

jokes.text: This contest is over. Give that man the $10,000!
jokes.setup: Hans Moleman getting hit by football
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate enthusiastic verdict
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's lowbrow taste in entertainment

jokes.text: This isn't America's Funniest Home Videos. But the ball, his groin. It works on so many levels.
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-intellectual analysis of groin injury
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on lowbrow humor with Homer's mock-intellectual defense

jokes.text: My name is Barney Gumble. I'm 40, I'm single, and I drink.
jokes.punchline: Stark, simple admission of his life problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment that combines humor with genuine pathos

jokes.text: There's a line in Othello about a drinker. Now a sensible man, by and by a fool, and presently a beast. That pretty well covers it.
jokes.punchline: Applying Shakespeare to his own alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development showing Barney's hidden intelligence and self-awareness

jokes.text: Excuse me. Did something crawl down your throat and die? It didn't die.
jokes.setup: Girl Scout asking about Barney's voice
jokes.punchline: Barney's grim correction about his condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Barney's physical deterioration from drinking

jokes.text: Mr. Gumble, this is a Girl Scout meeting. Is it? Or is it that you girls can't admit you have a problem?
jokes.setup: Girl Scout pointing out wrong meeting
jokes.punchline: Barney turning it into addiction intervention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment where Barney projects his addiction issues onto Girl Scouts

jokes.text: Don't cry for me. I'm already dead.
jokes.punchline: Existential statement about his condition
jokes.explanation: Iconic Barney line that perfectly balances comedy with genuine pathos, highly quotable

jokes.text: We did 20 takes, and that was the best one.
jokes.punchline: Director's admission of repeated failure
jokes.explanation: Good behind-the-scenes humor showing Burns' terrible acting ability

jokes.text: Smithers, are they booing me? No. They're saying, 'Boo-urns, Boo-urns.'
jokes.punchline: Smithers' creative reinterpretation of obvious booing
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons moment that became a widely-quoted meme, perfect character dynamics

jokes.text: I was saying, 'Boo-urns.'
jokes.punchline: One person actually supporting Smithers' explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the iconic gag with typical Moleman character

jokes.text: Coming, Eudora!
jokes.punchline: Jay actually has a date with the elderly author
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals Jay's romantic life is as bizarre as suggested

jokes.text: Like the Miss Teen America pageant.
jokes.setup: Burns noting that bribing judges paid off
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to bribing beauty pageant
jokes.explanation: Typical Burns casual admission of corruption

jokes.text: Let's just say it moved me to a bigger house!
jokes.setup: Asked how he could vote for Burns' movie
jokes.punchline: Blatant admission of bribery
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor showing Quimby's casual acceptance of bribes

jokes.text: I said the quiet part loud and the loud part quiet. Oh, dear.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his mistake
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about political speech and corruption that became widely quoted

jokes.text: Barney's movie had heart. But Football in the Groin had a football in the groin.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about obvious physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic that shows his struggle between sentiment and slapstick

jokes.text: Barney's movie had heart. But Football in the Groin had a football in the groin.
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple but accurate analysis of each film's appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - acknowledges art but prefers simple physical comedy

jokes.text: Wow, I'll never drink another beer. Beer here. I'll take 10.
jokes.punchline: Immediate relapse at beer offer
jokes.explanation: Classic addiction comedy showing the impossibility of Barney's recovery

jokes.text: You know something, Marge? It's not that tough being a film cricket.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on his jury experience
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'critic' as 'cricket'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer malapropism showing his newfound confidence

jokes.text: Congratulations, Barney. And enjoy your grand prize... a lifetime supply of Duff Beer. / Just hook it to my veins!
jokes.setup: Barney wins film festival after making movie about alcoholism
jokes.punchline: Prize is beer, Barney wants it intravenously
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic prize for reformed alcoholic with extreme delivery

jokes.text: Just hook it to my veins!
jokes.setup: Barney winning lifetime supply of Duff Beer
jokes.punchline: Requesting intravenous beer delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment that escalates his addiction to medical extremes

jokes.text: This has spring snakes inside, but the suckers will think it's beer nuts. Beer nuts.
jokes.punchline: Simple prank toy while mispronouncing item twice
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment showing his idea of sophisticated humor

jokes.text: Marge, look. This has spring snakes inside, but the suckers will think it's beer nuts. / Beer nuts.
jokes.setup: Homer showing Jay novelty can to prove small-town people aren't dumb
jokes.punchline: Jay immediately falls for the prank
jokes.explanation: Good irony about intelligence assumptions with immediate payoff

jokes.text: I've got to win this one. I bribed everyone in Hollywood.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of massive corruption
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment showing his expectation that money solves everything

jokes.text: George C. Scott in Man Getting Hit by Football.
jokes.punchline: Prestigious actor in lowbrow slapstick winning Oscar
jokes.explanation: Great escalation callback that brings the lowbrow movie to Hollywood prestige
